summary: Build 201 is a LOCAL-ONLY application assessment and fastest-path-to-live recommendation. It reviews the RoofLeadHQ repo, evidence chain, build history, readiness summaries, and launch blockers, and recommends the fastest, smartest, safest way to get the application to a useful live pilot. It performs NO live action: no SMS, no Twilio, no network/external call, no roofer/homeowner contact, no secrets/tokens/SIDs/phone numbers/production data, no live automation, no schema/auth/RLS changes. Names/booleans/codes/counts only. Safety posture preserved: demo_ready_with_live_automation_disabled.

blocked.note: Live sends are gated behind a Jason-operated manual action BY DESIGN; this is the safety posture, not a defect.

fastest_path_to_live.minimum_viable_live_pilot_path: One real roofer + Jason-operated manual lead qualification + one approved SMS template + existing opt-out/quiet-hours safety + manual booking. No new automation.

next_steps_3_to_7.action: Run the already-approved Build 200 one-message expansion send in your shell (fail-closed runner, one message, no retry) to prove a real controlled send succeeds with the validated destination.
Single LOCAL-ONLY closeout build capturing that send outcome (names/codes only), mirroring Build 196/199 — do NOT split into multiple builds.
Onboard ONE real consenting roofer via scripts/onboard-roofer.sh; confirm SMS template + opt-out/quiet-hours config.
Take ONE real homeowner lead, qualify it manually, send ONE approved SMS via the same fail-closed/manual path in your controlled shell.
One consolidated pilot-result evidence build (intake -> outreach -> outcome), not one build per state.
Go/No-Go review against the launch gating checklist; decide whether to widen to a second roofer or harden automation.

launch_gating_recommendation.rollback_stop_procedure: Stop sending immediately; do not retry; preserve outcome as names/codes only; capture a LOCAL-ONLY closeout; Jason owns stop/rollback.

build_recommendation.single_best_next_build_after_201: Build 202 = ONE consolidated LOCAL-ONLY closeout of the Jason-operated Build 200 expansion retry send (capture outcome names/codes only, update launch-readiness, single verifier+wrapper) — only AFTER Jason actually runs the send. If Jason instead pauses expansion, substitute branch B (one feedback+backlog packet); if going straight to a real pilot, substitute branch C (one consolidated pilot launch packet).

top_5_recommendations: Stop the per-state fixture-ceremony loop; ONE consolidated pilot packet per controlled milestone, not many micro-builds for the same narrow SMS use case.
Have Jason execute the already-approved Build 200 one-message send (or explicitly decide to stop expansion) to convert sunk approval into a real learning, then close out once locally.
Onboard ONE consenting roofer/test participant and run ONE real SMS interaction end-to-end manually before building any more automation.
Finalize homeowner SMS consent/opt-out language and keep messaging honest (book inspections, not guaranteed jobs; no live billing/CRM/quote claims) before any real-homeowner message.

Cut build/token/permission overhead: one reusable verifier over a manifest, one living readiness doc, run Claude in --permission-mode auto scoped to /root/roofleadhq, and keep secrets in a local secret file outside the repo.

supplemental_business_context.core_promise: Close the gap between a roofing lead and a booked inspection: respond fast, qualify, route/flag, move toward an inspection appointment, and give the contractor reporting/visibility.
supplemental_business_context.target_customer: Small-to-mid roofing contractors who miss leads, respond slowly, lack follow-up discipline, or want better intake/reporting.
supplemental_business_context.initial_pilot_shape: Very narrow: one consenting roofer/test identity, SMS only, one controlled interaction at a time.

decision_branches.branch_a_continue_expansion: Jason runs the already-approved Build 200 expansion retry once; then ONE consolidated local-only closeout (Build 202).
decision_branches.branch_b_pause_expansion: Stop expansion; build ONE post-pilot customer-feedback + product-priority backlog packet instead of more send scaffolding.
decision_branches.branch_c_first_real_pilot: Build ONE consolidated pilot launch packet (consent + one-message scope + manual send + observation form + rollback rules + go/no-go) for a single consenting roofer.
decision_branches.recommended_sequence: A then C: run the already-approved send to bank a real result and close it out in one build, then go straight to ONE consolidated pilot packet (branch C). Use branch B only if the controlled send teaches that the workflow needs product changes first.
decision_branches.do_not: Do not re-run the approve/preflight/closeout trio as separate builds for the same narrow SMS use case.

minimum_viable_pilot_framing.goal: A useful live pilot, NOT a perfect product or unrestricted launch.
minimum_viable_pilot_framing.shortest_path_to_learning_from_a_roofer: Run one controlled SMS interaction, observe delivery + response + usefulness, capture feedback manually, then decide proceed/pause.
